L1.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists in the content rendering pipeline where a document inclusion mechanism fails to properly scope execution privileges. Included content executes with the rights of a privileged system author rather than the requesting user's permissions. An authenticated low-privilege user can exploit this to achieve arbitrary code execution on the server by injecting scripting macros that inherit elevated privileges through the flawed author attribution.

ground_truth.reason: The class sheet document inclusion does not enforce author-scoped execution, allowing any authenticated user to execute arbitrary scripting macros with the privileges of the sheet's author.
